subject: Konus-Wind detection of GRB 251103A
body:
D. Svinkin, D. Frederiks, A. Lysenko, A. Ridnaia,
A. Tsvetkova, M. Ulanov, and T. Cline
on behalf of the Konus-Wind team, report: 

The long-duration GRB 251103A
(SVOM-ECLAIRs detection: Hu et al., GCN 42534;
Fermi-GBM detection: The Fermi GBM team, GCN 42536;
Sonawane and Godwin, GCN 42553;
SVOM-GRM detection: Wang et al., GCN 42552;
Insight-HXMT/HE detection: Ren et al., GCN 42572)
triggered Konus-Wind at T0=17189.335 s UT (04:46:29.335).

The burst light curve shows a single pulse
which starts at ~T0-0.5 s and has a total duration of ~5.5 s.
The emission is seen up to ~2 MeV.

The Konus-Wind light curve of this GRB is available at
http://www.ioffe.ru/LEA/GRBs/GRB251103_T17189/

As observed by Konus-Wind, the burst
had the total fluence of 9.48(-0.93,+1.02)x10^-6 erg/cm2,
and the 64-ms peak flux, measured from T0+0.022 s,
of 6.52(-2.04,+2.79)x10^-6 erg/cm2/s
(both in the 20 keV - 10 MeV energy range).

The time-averaged spectrum of the burst
(measured from T0 to T0+8.448 s)
is best fit in the 20 keV - 15 MeV range
by the GRB (Band) model with the following parameters:
the low-energy photon index alpha = -0.33(-0.71,+1.08),
the high energy photon index beta = -2.21(-0.11,+0.09),
the peak energy Ep = 86(-15,+25) keV
(chi2 = 86/80 dof).

The spectrum near the maximum count rate
(measured from T0 to T0+0.256 s)
is best fit in the 20 keV - 2 MeV range
by the GRB (Band) model with the following parameters:
the low-energy photon index alpha = -0.97(-0.42,+0.99),
the high energy photon index beta = -2.25(-7.75,+0.39),
the peak energy Ep = 292(-160,+184) keV
(chi2 = 16/18 dof).

Assuming the redshift z=0.76 (Izzo et al., GCN 42540)
and a standard cosmology with H_0 = 67.3 km/s/Mpc, Omega_M = 0.315,
and Omega_Lambda = 0.685 (Planck Collaboration, 2014),
we estimate the following rest-frame parameters:
the isotropic energy release E_iso is 1.53(-0.15,+0.16)x10^52 erg,
the peak luminosity L_iso is 1.85(-0.58,+0.79)x10^52 erg/s,
the rest-frame peak energy of the time-averaged spectrum
Ep,i,z is 151(-27,+44) keV and the spectrum near the maximum count rate
Ep,p,z is 513(-281,+324) keV.

With the obtained estimates, GRB 251103A is inside 68% prediction bands 
for both the 'Amati' and the 'Yonetoku' relations derived for the sample 
of >300 long KW GRBs with known redshifts (Tsvetkova et al., 2017; Tsvetkova et al., 2021),
see http://www.ioffe.ru/LEA/GRBs/GRB251103_T17189/GRB251103A_rest_frame.pdf

All the quoted errors are at the 68% confidence level.
All the quoted values are preliminary.
